title: Chylismia parryi
taxonomy: Chylismia parryi is classified within the family Onagraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. This classification places it within the order Myrtales and the clade Eurosids II of the angiosperms. At the rank of genus, it is classified as Chylismia, which is a group of flowering plants. The species Chylismia parryi, also known as Parry's evening primrose, is native to the southwestern region of the United States. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics between Chylismia parryi and other species within the family Onagraceae.
uses: Chylismia parryi, also known as the Parry's primrose, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant was used to make teas and infusions, which were believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. However, there is limited scientific evidence to support these claims. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Chylismia parryi.
benefits: Chylismia parryi, also known as the desert five-spot, is a wildflower native to the southwestern United States. constituents: Chylismia parryi, also known as the Desert Gold, contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides. parts: Chylismia parryi, also known as Parry's primrose, has been traditionally used by the indigenous peoples of the southwestern United States and Mexico for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and stems are the primary parts used for medicinal purposes. intro: Chylismia parryi, also known as Parry's evening primrose, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The health benefits of Chylismia parryi include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chylismia parryi include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
